frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current percepts are biased toward recently presented stimuli

frameworks.name: temporal continuity exploitation
frameworks.one_line: perceptual systems leverage temporal redundancy for efficiency

core_question: Does serial dependence arise within perception itself or at later decision and response stages?
key_assumptions: sequential stimuli produce biases toward previous stimulus
perceptual, decision, and response stages can be experimentally dissociated
manipulations: dissociation of stimulus from response across trials
range of orientation differences spanning predicted maximal effect
key_findings: strong serial dependence observed in orientation judgements
serial dependence present in both perceptual and decision processes
very little contribution from the response stage
dependence arises between stimuli, not responses
interpretations: serial dependence acts directly on perception itself
the bias exploits temporal redundancies to enhance perceptual efficiency
